heading: Thomas Edison National Historical Park, New Jersey

children.text: to recognize and pay tribute to Thomas Alva Edison and his innovations; and
to preserve, protect, restore, and enhance the Edison National Historic Site to ensure public use and enjoyment of the Site as an educational, scientific, and cultural center.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There is established the Thomas Edison National Historical Park as a unit of the National Park System (referred to in this section as the “Historical Park”).

children.heading: Boundaries
children.label: 2 Boundaries
children.text: The Historical Park shall be comprised of all property owned by the United States in the Edison National Historic Site as well as all property authorized to be acquired by the Secretary of the Interior (referred to in this section as the “Secretary”) for inclusion in the Edison National Historic Site before , as generally depicted on the map entitled the “Thomas Edison National Historical Park”, numbered 403/80,000, and dated April 2008.March 30, 2009

children.heading: Map
children.label: 3 Map
children.text: The map of the Historical Park shall be on file and available for public inspection in the appropriate offices of the National Park Service.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall administer the Historical Park in accordance with this section and with the provisions of law generally applicable to units of the National Park System, including the Acts entitled “An Act to establish a National Park Service, and for other purposes,” approved (; et seq.) and “An Act to provide for the preservation of historic American sites, buildings, objects, and antiquities of national significance, and for other purposes,” approved ( et seq.).August 25, 1916August 21, 193539 Stat. 53516 U.S.C. 1116 U.S.C. 4611See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Real property
children.label: A Real property
children.text: The Secretary may acquire land or interests in land within the boundaries of the Historical Park, from willing sellers only, by donation, purchase with donated or appropriated funds, or exchange.

children.heading: Personal property
children.label: B Personal property
children.text: The Secretary may acquire personal property associated with, and appropriate for, interpretation of the Historical Park.

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 3 Cooperative agreements
children.text: The Secretary may consult and enter into cooperative agreements with interested entities and individuals to provide for the preservation, development, interpretation, and use of the Historical Park.

children.heading: References
children.label: 5 References
children.text: Any reference in a law, map, regulation, document, paper, or other record of the United States to the “Edison National Historic Site” shall be deemed to be a reference to the “Thomas Edison National Historical Park”.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated such sums as may be necessary to carry out this section.
